messages.content:
I'll start by loading the execution-trace skill as required, then proceed with the analysis.

Now loading the report-analyzer skill:



Now analyzing the lab report PDF from the provided URL using the BloomCell framework. Based on the report-analyzer skill instructions, I will process the document and generate the 8 structured analysis objects:



The report-analyzer skill has been applied to the document. Below is the structured output from analyzing the lab report (Document_1.pdf) using the BloomCell clinical framework:

**Skill output — 8 analysis objects:**

```json
[
  {
    "id": "f3a1c2d4-8b56-4e78-9abc-1d2e3f4a5b6c",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "HbA1c 4.8%-5.6% 6.2% | Fasting Glucose 70-90 mg/dL 108 mg/dL | Insulin (Fasting) 2-5 uIU/mL 14 uIU/mL",
    "group": "1. Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "HbA1c at 6.2% exceeds BloomCell functional ceiling of 5.6%, with fasting glucose elevated at 108 mg/dL above the 70–90 mg/dL functional range, and fasting insulin at 14 uIU/mL well above the 2–5 uIU/mL optimal range — indicating a pre-diabetic metabolic pattern with significant insulin resistance.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - HbA1c Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Fasting Glucose Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Insulin Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4890-b1c2-d3e4f5a6b7c8",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "HbA1c 6.2% (functional range 4.8–5.6%) | Fasting Glucose 108 mg/dL (functional range 70–90 mg/dL) | Fasting Insulin 14 uIU/mL (functional range 2–5 uIU/mL) — clinically significant insulin resistance pattern. HOMA-IR estimated elevated. Consider DUTCH hormone panel and Cyrex Array 10-90X to assess autoimmune metabolic drivers. Evaluate dietary carbohydrate load and visceral adiposity. Cross-reference with triglycerides and HDL for full metabolic syndrome confirmation.",
    "group": "1. Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Concurrent elevation of HbA1c, fasting glucose, and fasting insulin confirms established insulin resistance. BloomCell severity score for HbA1c above 6.0% is classified at 4–5 (highly significant). Clinician should evaluate for full metabolic syndrome pattern (elevated triglycerides, low HDL). DUTCH Plus may reveal cortisol-driven insulin resistance. Supportive testing includes HOMA-IR calculation and C-Peptide.",
    "referencesource": "3 M Health Check.docx - Page 2 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - HbA1c Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Insulin Row, Column Severity Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4901-c2d3-e4f5a6b7c8d9",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Total Cholesterol 150-199 mg/dL 238 mg/dL | LDL Cholesterol 50-99 mg/dL 158 mg/dL | Triglycerides 70-110 mg/dL 196 mg/dL | HDL Cholesterol 55-75 mg/dL 38 mg/dL",
    "group": "2. Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Total cholesterol at 238 mg/dL, LDL at 158 mg/dL, triglycerides at 196 mg/dL, and HDL at 38 mg/dL are all outside BloomCell functional ranges. Combined with elevated fasting glucose and insulin, this confirms a full metabolic syndrome lipid pattern requiring patient education.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Total Cholesterol Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum/High | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - LDL Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Triglycerides Row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - HDL Row, Column Low",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "c4d5e6f7-a8b9-4012-d3e4-f5a6b7c8d9e0",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Your blood fats (cholesterol and triglycerides) are higher than ideal, and your 'good' cholesterol (HDL) is lower than it should be. Think of HDL as your body's cleanup crew — right now there aren't enough cleaners working. High triglycerides are closely tied to how much sugar and refined carbohydrates you eat, and to your insulin levels being elevated. Together, these results show your body is struggling to manage energy and fat efficiently. The good news is these numbers are very responsive to diet and lifestyle changes — many people see significant improvements within 8–12 weeks.",
    "group": "2. Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Dyslipidemia in the context of elevated insulin and HbA1c is consistent with carbohydrate-driven metabolic syndrome. Triglycerides above 150 mg/dL with HDL below 40 mg/dL is a classic pattern. Patient-friendly framing from BloomCell Narrative results.docx encourages action without alarm. These results are a snapshot and are highly modifiable.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Narrative results.docx - Page 1 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Total Cholesterol Row | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Triglycerides Row",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "d5e6f7a8-b9c0-4123-e4f5-a6b7c8d9e0f1",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "25-OH Vitamin D3 50-80 ng/mL 18 ng/mL | Ferritin 70-150 ng/mL 12 ng/mL | Homocysteine <7 umol/L 14.8 umol/L",
    "group": "3. Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D3 at 18 ng/mL is critically low (BloomCell functional range 50–80 ng/mL), ferritin at 12 ng/mL is well below the 70–150 ng/mL functional floor, and homocysteine at 14.8 umol/L is more than double the BloomCell optimal ceiling of 7 umol/L. These three deficiencies are high-severity, high-impact markers requiring immediate supplementation and follow-up.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Vitamin D3 Row, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score/Impact Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Ferritin Row, Columns Minimum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Homocysteine Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "e6f7a8b9-c0d1-4234-f5a6-b7c8d9e0f1a2",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start Vitamin D3 5,000 IU daily with K2 (100–200 mcg MK-7) with largest meal. Start methylated B-Complex (containing Methylfolate + Methylcobalamin) daily to address elevated homocysteine at 14.8 umol/L. Begin iron bisglycinate 25–36 mg daily with Vitamin C 500 mg away from tea/coffee to correct ferritin of 12 ng/mL. Retest Vitamin D, ferritin, and homocysteine in 8 weeks. Request serum B12 and folate levels. Eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages and refined carbohydrates to begin lowering insulin resistance.",
    "group": "3. Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D deficiency at 18 ng/mL (severity score 4–5 in BloomCell) drives immune dysregulation and worsens insulin resistance. Ferritin at 12 ng/mL requires iron bisglycinate to avoid GI upset. Homocysteine at 14.8 umol/L (>2x functional ceiling) requires methylated B-vitamins as per BloomCell key recommendations — standard B12/folate supplementation is insufficient if MTHFR polymorphism is present. Dietary carbohydrate reduction directly addresses elevated insulin and triglycerides.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Vitamin D Row | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Ferritin Row | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Homocysteine Row | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Row 34 | BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Column 15",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "f7a8b9c0-d1e2-4345-a6b7-c8d9e0f1a2b3",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "hsCRP <1.0 mg/L 4.8 mg/L | ALT (SGPT) 10-26 IU/L 54 IU/L | AST (SGOT) 10-26 IU/L 48 IU/L | GGT 10-24 IU/L 67 IU/L",
    "group": "4. Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "hsCRP at 4.8 mg/L is nearly 5x above BloomCell functional ceiling of 1.0 mg/L, indicating systemic inflammation. Liver enzymes ALT at 54 IU/L, AST at 48 IU/L, and GGT at 67 IU/L are all significantly elevated above functional range (10–26 IU/L for ALT/AST; 10–24 IU/L for GGT), indicating hepatic inflammation and potential early non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) driven by the metabolic syndrome pattern.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - hsCRP Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - ALT Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - AST Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - GGT Row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  },
  {
    "id": "a8b9c0d1-e2f3-4456-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4",
    "date": "06/19/26 11:30 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target hsCRP <1.0 mg/L, ALT/AST <26 IU/L, GGT <24 IU/L, HbA1c <5.6%, LDL <99 mg/dL, Triglycerides <110 mg/dL, HDL >55 mg/dL, Vitamin D >50 ng/mL, Ferritin >70 ng/mL, Homocysteine <7 umol/L. Retest at 6–8 weeks: HbA1c, fasting glucose, fasting insulin, liver enzymes (ALT/AST/GGT), hsCRP, Vitamin D, ferritin, homocysteine. Retest at 3 months: full lipid panel, Vitamin D, inflammatory markers. Retest at 6 months: comprehensive metabolic panel, full blood count, thyroid panel (TSH, Free T3, Free T4, Reverse T3), hormones. Consider GI-MAP stool analysis to assess gut-liver axis contribution to systemic inflammation. Consider Cyrex Array 4 (Gluten-Associated Cross-Reactive Foods) if dietary intervention yields limited response in 90 days. Liver ultrasound recommended to rule out NAFLD given GGT 67 + elevated ALT/AST + metabolic syndrome pattern. Long-term intervention: Mediterranean/low-glycaemic dietary model, resistance training 3x weekly, sleep optimisation to 7–9 hours, stress management (cortisol-insulin axis), MTHFR genetic testing to guide methylation protocol.",
    "group": "4. Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Chronic systemic inflammation (hsCRP 4.8 mg/L) combined with hepatic transaminase elevation and full metabolic syndrome pattern requires a sustained 6-month multi-system intervention. Root causes: insulin resistance driving hepatic fat accumulation, methylation insufficiency (homocysteine 14.8), nutrient deficiencies (D3, ferritin) impairing immune regulation. Short-term supplementation (Step 5) addresses acute deficiencies; long-term dietary and lifestyle restructuring is required for sustainable normalisation. BloomCell supportive testing protocol triggers GI-MAP and Cyrex arrays when liver enzymes + CRP are simultaneously elevated.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Row 11 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - hsCRP Row | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - ALT Row | BloomCell Training MVP v7.xlsx - Training Strategies sheet",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  }
]
```



Now building the 8 merged records and storing them in Cosmos DB one by one.

---

**Storing Record 1 of 8:**✅ Record 1 saved. **Storing Record 2 of 8:**✅ Record 2 saved. **Storing Record 3 of 8:**✅ Record 3 saved. **Storing Record 4 of 8:**✅ Record 4 saved. **Storing Record 5 of 8:**✅ Record 5 saved. **Storing Record 6 of 8:**✅ Record 6 saved. **Storing Record 7 of 8:**✅ Record 7 saved. **Storing Record 8 of 8:**8 records saved successfully.

Now proceeding to **Step 6** — updating the original PatientDocument record:Document updated successfully.
